spec_match.rationale: Renders all four font-variant-emoji values against the dual-presentation Unicode characters (U+FE0E/U+FE0F codepoints). Exact use case the spec calls out.

interactive.rationale: Four columns shown side by side, no live input or toggling. Visitor can compare but cannot type their own characters in or interact with the variant choice.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises all four values side by side covering symbol-font and user-content cases; page delivers a four-way matrix.

edge_cases.rationale: Static set of chars only; no demonstration of how `font-variant-emoji: unicode` resolves the U+FE0E/FE0F variation selectors automatically (the key spec behaviour difference between `unicode` and `normal`).

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, no static-card, columns use design system tokens.

openQuestions.title: Add a live-edit input
openQuestions.detail: Let the visitor type/paste their own Unicode characters and see all four variants render simultaneously. Static columns underwhelm — interactive composition is the headline for this feature.

openQuestions.title: Explain how `unicode` value differs from `normal`
openQuestions.detail: The `unicode` value respects U+FE0E/FE0F selectors; `normal` uses the platform default. Add an annotation column or tooltip showing the precedence relationship — currently visitors can't tell from the rendered output why the value matters.

summary: Resolved the required ChromeStatus reference; 2 follow-up question(s) remain.
